chunks.page_summary: This page explains the viscosity units available in the Rheonics RPS System, how they are used, and the steps to select the desired unit for measurement reports.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

RPS cup seconds conversion

Modified on: Tue, 17 Dec, 2024 at 10:20 PM

What products are involved?
RPS Rheonics Process Systems: InkSight, SlurryTrack, PaintTrack
(https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/)

What is the purpose of this article?
To explain how engineering units behave in the RPS System Screen and when the Cup Seconds Conversion function is working

1. Which viscosity units are available on RPS System?

Rheonics RPS software can display 3 different units for viscosity: mPa.s, cP and Seconds (s). The first two units are equivalent and will show the direct viscosity measurement from Rheonics sensor. The Seconds Unit is used for a Rheonics to Viscosity Cup conversion feature. This is useful to compare the Rheonics sensor readings with Viscosity Cups e.g. Zahn cups.

Real-time viscosity measurements can be visualized from the Control tab (Tab name can change depending on RPS System). Measurement units are not displayed in this Tab, just the values.

Figure 1. Color control tab Inksight software

2. Selecting the viscosity unit

Follow next steps to select the viscosity unit in the RPS System. This viscosity value will ultimately be saved in the measurement reports.

1. Go to the Settings Tab.
2. Select the required units of viscosity.
key_facts: The article aims to explain the behavior of engineering units in the RPS System Screen and the functionality of the Cup Seconds Conversion.
Rheonics RPS software supports three viscosity units: milliPascal-seconds (mPa.s), centiPoise (cP), and Seconds (s).
mPa.s and cP are equivalent units for direct viscosity measurement from a Rheonics sensor.
The Seconds Unit is specifically used for a Rheonics to Viscosity Cup conversion feature, useful for comparing sensor readings with traditional viscosity cups like Zahn cups.
Real-time viscosity measurements are displayed as numerical values in the Control tab, without explicit units shown in that tab.
To select the desired viscosity unit in the RPS System, users must navigate to the Settings Tab.
The selected viscosity unit will be saved in all subsequent measurement reports.
diagram_labels: Figure 1. Color control tab Inksight software
warnings_notes: The 'Control tab' name can change depending on the specific RPS System configuration.
Measurement units are not displayed in the Control tab; only the numerical values are shown.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on configuring viscosity units, temperature units, and cup equivalent settings, along with managing conversion coefficients for Rheonics inline viscometers and understanding their correlation with traditional viscosity cup measurements.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 2. Viscosity unit selection.

3. If the selected value is cP or mPa.s then the viscosity value shown in Control Tab will be the direct output form the Rheonics sensor. Consider that cP and mPa.s are equivalent units (1 to 1 relation).

4. In case you switch the viscosity units to Seconds as shown in Figure 3, then the cup-seconds conversion coefficients A1 and A0 will be used to calculate a cup-seconds equivalent for the Rheonics sensor measurement.

How does viscosity readings of Rheonics sensors correlate to viscosity cups readings?

There is not a direct relation that will hold true for all fluids between Rheonics sensors and an specific Viscosity measuring cup. Consider that most real application fluids are Non-Newtonian and that the response is dependent on the shear rate. Clients can evaluate whether there is a suitable correlation between inline viscosity readings and manual cup measurements. This is usually done by registering both instrument measurements and manual cup measurements.
Read more on this topic here: https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000410167-introduction-to-correlation-scale-up.

Figure 3. Cup-seconds conversion coefficients.

Rheonics pre calculated coefficients

You can import from a list of pre-calculated coefficients for different units or calculate your own as indicated in this article: Converting inline viscosity measurement to equivalent cup seconds https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000402730-cup-second-conversion-and-coefficients-for-rheonics-srv These values will not hold true for all fluids and it is recommended that clients verify the correctness of coefficients by testing with the fluid of interest.

5. You can input your custom values in the Settings. For example, if A1= 0.36 and A0=11, you can write the values directly in the coefficients box as shown next.
key_facts: If cP or mPa.s are selected, the viscosity value in the Control Tab is the direct output from the Rheonics sensor.
cP and mPa.s are equivalent units with a 1 to 1 relation.
If viscosity units are switched to Seconds, the cup-seconds conversion coefficients A1 and A0 are used to calculate a cup-seconds equivalent for the Rheonics sensor measurement.
There is not a direct relation that holds true for all fluids between Rheonics sensors and any specific Viscosity measuring cup.
Most real application fluids are Non-Newtonian, and their response is dependent on the shear rate.
Clients can evaluate whether there is a suitable correlation between inline viscosity readings and manual cup measurements.
It is recommended that clients verify the correctness of coefficients by testing with the fluid of interest, as pre-calculated values may not hold true for all fluids.
Custom A1 and A0 values can be directly input in the Settings.
warnings_notes: There is not a direct relation that will hold true for all fluids between Rheonics sensors and an specific Viscosity measuring cup.
Consider that most real application fluids are Non-Newtonian and that the response is dependent on the shear rate.
These values will not hold true for all fluids and it is recommended that clients verify the correctness of coefficients by testing with the fluid of interest.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes how to input A0 and A1 coefficients for cup-second viscosity conversion in Rheonics software and presents a table of calculated cup-second equivalent values.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 4. A1 coefficients for the cup-second conversion input on the software.
Cup-second conversion coefficients
S= A1: 0.14 x cP + A0: 36 cSt

Figure 5. A0 coefficients for the cup-second conversion on the software.
Cup-second conversion coefficients
S= A1: 0.36 x cP + A0: 36 cSt

And that's all. By writing these A0 and A1 values, and having the units selected as cup-seconds, the Control Tab will now show the cup-second equivalent for your cup.

Figure 6. Calculated values for cup-second conversion.

Table of Calculated Values:
# | Color ID | Lock | Auto | Status | Actual
--|----------|------|------|--------|-------
1 | 001      | Lock | Manual | (circle) | 40.5
2 | Stat2    | Lock | Manual | (circle) | 42.4
3 | 003      | Lock | Manual | (circle) | 40.5
4 | 004      | Lock | Manual | (circle) | 40.5
5 | 005      | Lock | Manual | (circle) | 40.5
6 | 006      | Lock | Manual | (circle) | 40.5

For further information review Rheonics RPS Manual or contact Support Team at support@rheonics.com

References
1. https://rheonics.com/products/rps-inksight-rheonics-printing-solution/
2. https://rheonics.com/slurrytrack/
key_facts: A0 and A1 coefficients are configurable inputs in the Rheonics software for cup-second viscosity conversion.
The conversion formula used is S = A1 * cP + A0, which calculates a value in cSt.
By setting A0 and A1 values and selecting cup-seconds as the units, the Control Tab will display the cup-second equivalent.
The page provides examples of calculated cup-second values for six different color IDs.
Further information can be obtained from the Rheonics RPS Manual or by contacting their support team.
diagram_labels: Figure 4. A1 coefficients for the cup-second conversion input on the software.
Figure 5. A0 coefficients for the cup-second conversion on the software.
Figure 6. Calculated values for cup-second conversion.
warnings_notes: For further information review Rheonics RPS Manual or contact Support Team at support@rheonics.com
